comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello @UtakataKyosui, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request significantly improves the application's performance and security by integrating WebAssembly for client-side image compression and upgrading password hashing to Argon2id. It also enhances user experience with a new theme toggling feature and improves overall accessibility across various forms. The changes are supported by updated dependencies, database migrations, and new documentation for best practices. ### Highlights * **WASM Image Compression**: Implemented client-side image compression for item uploads using the `@silvia-odwyer/photon` WebAssembly library, including image resizing and base64 encoding, to optimize upload performance. * **Enhanced Password Security**: Upgraded user password hashing from `Bun.password` to Argon2id using the `hash-wasm` library, providing a more robust and secure password storage mechanism. * **Theme Toggling Feature**: Introduced a new light/dark theme toggling functionality, complete with a `ThemeProvider` and `ThemeToggle` component, and server-side rendering support for initial theme detection. * **Database Schema Update**: Added an `image` column to the `items` table in the database schema to store compressed image data. * **Accessibility Improvements**: Enhanced form accessibility by adding `aria-describedby` attributes for error messages, explicit `for` attributes for labels, and `required` attributes for login fields. * **New Skill Documentation**: Added comprehensive documentation for HTML Best Practices & Accessibility and WebAssembly Performance Optimization, outlining guidelines and recommended libraries. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Changelog * **.agent/skills/html-accessibility-best-practices/SKILL.md** * Added new skill documentation outlining HTML best practices and accessibility guidelines. * **.agent/skills/wasm-performance-optimization/SKILL.md** * Added new skill documentation providing guidelines for WebAssembly performance optimization. * **bun.lock** * Updated dependencies to include `@silvia-odwyer/photon` for WASM image processing and `hash-wasm` for Argon2id hashing. * **drizzle/migrations/0004_many_unus.sql** * Added a new migration to introduce an `image` text column to the `items` table. * **drizzle/migrations/meta/0004_snapshot.json** * Updated the Drizzle ORM snapshot to reflect the addition of the `image` column in the `items` table. * **drizzle/migrations/meta/_journal.json** * Added a new migration entry for the `0004_many_unus` migration. * **drizzle/schema.ts** * Added an optional `image` text field to the `Items` schema definition. * **package.json** * Added `@silvia-odwyer/photon` and `hash-wasm` to the project dependencies. * **panda.config.ts** * Added a `canvas` semantic token to the color palette, likely for theme-aware backgrounds. * **src/api/item/server.ts** * Modified `createItem` and `updateItem` functions to store the `image` field from form data. * **src/api/server.ts** * Replaced `Bun.password.verify` with `argon2Verify` for user login authentication. * Replaced `Bun.password.hash` with `argon2id` for hashing new user passwords, including salt generation and specific Argon2id parameters. * Added a redirect to the login page if a user is not found after authentication. * **src/app.tsx** * Wrapped the application with `ThemeProvider` to enable global theme management. * Integrated `ThemeToggle` component into the navigation bar. * Updated navigation styling to be sticky, theme-aware, and converted `div` based navigation to a `ul` for semantic correctness. * **src/components/box/BoxForm.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form element for accessibility. * Added an `id` attribute to the storage select element for explicit labeling. * Casted `props.submission.result` to `Error` for type safety in error message display. * **src/components/category/CategoryForm.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form element for accessibility. * Casted `props.submission.result` to `Error` for type safety in error message display. * **src/components/item/ItemForm.tsx** * Implemented `createSignal` for `imagePreview`, `imageBase64`, and `isCompressing` states. * Added `handleFileChange` function to process image uploads using `@silvia-odwyer/photon` for compression and resizing. * Included a file input for image selection and a hidden input to submit the base64 compressed image. * Displayed an image preview and a 'Compressing...' status during image processing. * Disabled the submit button while image compression is in progress. * Added `aria-describedby` to the form element for accessibility. * Casted `props.submission.result` to `Error` for type safety in error message display. * **src/components/storage/StorageForm.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form element for accessibility. * Casted `props.submission.result` to `Error` for type safety in error message display. * **src/components/theme-provider.tsx** * Added a new `ThemeProvider` component to manage and persist the application's color mode (light/dark) using `localStorage` and `matchMedia`. * **src/components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx** * Added a new `ThemeToggle` component that uses `IconButton` and `lucide-solid` icons to switch between light and dark themes. * **src/entry-server.tsx** * Added an inline script to `entry-server.tsx` to apply the saved or system-preferred theme class and color scheme to the `documentElement` during server-side rendering, preventing FOUC. * **src/routes/login.tsx** * Added `aria-describedby` to the form element for accessibility. * Made username and password input fields `required`. * Casted `loggingIn.result` to `Error` for type safety in error message display. * **src/theme/conditions.ts** * Updated `light` condition and added a new `dark` condition to support theme-specific styling based on `data-theme` attribute.
Activity * The pull request introduces new features and refactors existing code, indicating active development. * New skill documentation files were added, suggesting an effort to document best practices. * Dependencies were updated, and database migrations were created, showing changes across multiple layers of the application.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for Github and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG). You can also get AI-powered code generation, chat, as well as code reviews directly in the IDE at no cost with the [Gemini Code Assist IDE Extension](https://cloud.google.com/products/gemini/code-assist).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).
